data.description:
Repro (verified on installed 0.4.0, temp store): create ticket, comment "only-once", copy the COMMENT event file to a new filename keeping the same uuid in the payload (ts+1s) -> show returns comments == ["only-once","only-once"].

Source: ticket_reducer/_api.py:197-244 — reduce_ticket accepts/defaults strategy=LastTimestampWinsStrategy() (docstring: "dedup by UUID (first occurrence wins), sort by timestamp") but NEVER calls strategy.resolve (verified: no .resolve( call sites in ticket_reducer/). _processors.py:347-416 replays raw files in FILENAME order with no UUID dedup (only snapshot-source-uuids skipped) and sorts by filename timestamp, not event timestamp. MostStatusEventsWinsStrategy is equally unreachable via the public API.

Impact: the documented merge-as-union convergence story leans on UUID-level determinism; duplicate-UUID files (recovery cherry-picks, manual copies, fsck-recover paths) silently double-apply.

Fix direction: either wire the strategy into replay (load -> strategy.resolve -> process) or delete the dead strategy classes/param and document filename-order replay; at minimum add a seen-UUID skip in replay_events. RED test: duplicate-UUID event under a second filename -> single application.
data.title: Reducer strategy is dead code: no UUID dedup on replay (duplicate-UUID events double-apply)
